info.title: Entities
info.description:
Productboard API v2 for managing the following Entities: 
  - product
  - component
  - feature
  - subfeature
  - initiative
  - objective
  - keyResult
  - release
  - releaseGroup
  - user
  - company

## Configuration-Driven API

This API is universal and respects the configuration of your Productboard workspace. 
The available fields, their types, and capabilities are determined by your workspace 
configuration, permissions, and business logic.

## Field Discovery

To discover what fields are available for each entity type and their capabilities, 
use the `/v2/entities/configuration` endpoint. This introspection endpoint returns:
- Available fields (both standard and custom)
- Field types and validation rules
- Supported operations (read, create, update)
- Available patch operations (set, addItems, removeItems, clear)
- Required vs optional fields

## Universal Entity Support

The API provides a unified interface for all listed entities. Each entity type 
supports the same core operations (create, read, update, delete, search) while respecting 
the specific configuration and field definitions for that entity type in your workspace.

## Dynamic Field System

Custom fields are identified by UUIDs and can be of various types (text, number, 
boolean, status, member, teams, tags, single_select, multi_select, rich_text, 
calculated, date, health, work_progress). The exact fields available depend on 
your workspace configuration.

tags.name: entities
tags.description: A resource for managing PM entities.

get.description:
Note: this endpoint is deprecated, use `/v2/entities/configurations/{type}` instead.

Returns metadata for a specific Entities type, including supported fields, patch operations, and validation rules.  
Use this to programmatically discover which fields are required, what types are available, and how your workspace is configured.

Returns metadata for all available Entities type, including supported fields, patch operations, and validation rules.  
Use this to programmatically discover what entity types are available for your workspace, which fields are required,
what types are available, and how your workspace is configured.

parameters.name: type[]
parameters.description: You can specify multiple entity types for filtering.

get.description:
Returns metadata for a specific Entities type, including supported fields, patch operations, and validation rules.  
Use this to programmatically discover which fields are required, what types are available, and how your workspace is configured.

post.description:
Creates a new Entities of the specified type.  
You must specify the `type` (e.g. `feature`, `initiative`) and provide required fields based on your workspace configuration.

Use the `/configuration` endpoint to discover what fields are available and required.

## Limitations
- The field names and availability depend on your workspace setup.

get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of Entities.
You can filter results, sort them, and control which fields are returned.

> Without the `members:pii:read` scope, owner email fields are returned as `[redacted]`. Filtering by `owner[email]` requires the `members:pii:read` scope. Without the `users:pii:read` scope, user entity name and email fields are returned as `[redacted]`.

Use the `type[]` query parameter to filter by one or more entity types (e.g. `type[]=feature&type[]=initiative`).
Use the `fields` parameter to request only the fields you need.

## Filter availability
Not all filters apply to all entity types. For example, `status` filtering is only available
for types that have a status field (e.g. `feature`, `initiative`), and `parent[id]` only works
for types that have a parent relationship.

Use `GET /v2/entities/configurations/{type}` to check which filters are supported for a specific type.
The `filters` array in the configuration response lists all available filter parameters,
their exact query parameter names, and value schemas.

## Limitations
- Only the `AND` operator is supported when applying multiple filters. `OR`, `NOT`, or nested conditions are not supported.

parameters.name: type[]
parameters.description:
Filter by one or more entity types. Use array notation: `type[]=feature&type[]=initiative`.
Currently supported PM entity types:
  - product
  - component
  - feature
  - subfeature
  - initiative
  - objective
  - keyResult
  - release
  - releaseGroup
  - user
  - company

The exact types available may vary based on the configuration of the workspace.

parameters.name: parent[id]
parameters.description: Filter by parent PM entity ID

get.description:
Retrieves all configured fields and values for the specified Entities.
Use this endpoint to load complete data for a single entity.

> Without the `members:pii:read` scope, owner email is returned as `[redacted]`. Without the `users:pii:read` scope, user entity name and email fields are returned as `[redacted]`.

You can limit the fields returned using the `fields` query parameter.

patch.description:
Updates one or more fields of a Entities.  
Use `fields` for full replacement of values, or `patch` for granular changes like adding/removing items in list-type fields.  
You must use either `fields` or `patch` – not both.

The `patch` property supports these operations:
- `set` – same as setting a value via `fields`
- `addItems` – add one or more values to a list field
- `removeItems` – remove values from a list field
- `clear` – erase the value of the field

## Limitations
- Providing both `fields` and `patch` in the same request will return an error.

delete.description:
Deletes a Product Management entity by ID. This operation permanently removes the specified entity from your workspace.

If the entity has any child entities (e.g. a product with components, or a feature with subfeatures), those children will be **deleted automatically** as part of a cascading delete.

**Use this endpoint with caution — deletion is irreversible.**

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the PM entity to delete.

post.description:
Performs a filtered search over Entities using a POST request.
This endpoint supports the same filtering capabilities as `GET /v2/entities`, but allows a larger and more complex payload.
It's useful when filtering by a long list of foreign key IDs or when URL length limits become a problem.

## Filter availability
Not all filters apply to all entity types. For example, `status` filtering is only available
for types that have a status field (e.g. `feature`, `initiative`), and `parent` filtering only works
for types that have a parent relationship.

Use `GET /v2/entities/configurations/{type}` to check which filters are supported for a specific type.
The `filters` array in the configuration response lists all available filter parameters,
their exact query parameter names, and value schemas.

> Filtering by `owner[email]` requires the `members:pii:read` scope.

## Limitations
- Only the `AND` operator is supported when applying multiple filters. `OR`, `NOT`, or nested conditions are not supported.
- You can search only within one entity type at a time.

get.description:
Retrieves all relationships associated with a specific Entities.
This includes parent, child, and linked entities.
Use this endpoint to understand how an entity is connected within the product hierarchy or planning structure.

The source entity is implicit (this entity).

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The type of the relationship to filter by (optional)

parameters.name: target[type]
parameters.description: The entity type of the target to filter relationships by (optional)

parameters.name: target[id]
parameters.description: The ID of the target entity to filter the relationships by (optional)

post.description:
Creates a relationship between two Entities.  
Specify the source entity via the URL path (`{id}`), and the target entity and relationship type in the request body.

The `type` field determines the direction and nature of the relationship.  
Supported values:
- `parent` – declare the target as the parent of the source
- `child` – declare the target as the child of the source
- `link` – create a non-hierarchical connection between entities
- `isBlockedBy` – create a dependency between entities
- `isBlocking` – create a dependency between entities

## Limitations
- Only valid combinations of source and target entities are allowed for each relationship type.
- The target entity must exist and be accessible.

put.description:
Replaces the parent of a specific Entities.  
Some entity types (such as features and subfeatures) are required to have a parent.  
This endpoint allows you to update the parent entity when the relationship changes.

The new parent is specified in the request body using the `target.id` field.

## Limitations
- Only valid parent relationships are allowed (e.g. feature → component, subfeature → feature).
- The target entity must exist and be a valid parent type.
- This will overwrite any existing parent relationship.

delete.description:
Removes a relationship between two Entities.  
Use the `type` query parameter to indicate which kind of relationship you want to remove.

parameters.name: targetId
parameters.description: The ID of the target entity to delete the relationship with.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of allowed values for select-type and status fields (SINGLE_SELECT, MULTI_SELECT, STATUS).
Use this endpoint to retrieve all available options for fields with many values, or when you need
to paginate through all values.

For fields with fewer values (≤1000), the values are returned inline in the field response.
For fields with more values, the field response contains a reference to this endpoint.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Field identifier (UUID for custom fields, API name for default fields).

parameters.name: assignedEntityType[]
parameters.description: Filter values by assigned entity type. Useful for status fields where different entity types have different status sets.

BearerAuth.description: API token authentication. Use your API token as the value of the Authorization header

OAuth2.description: OAuth 2.0 authentication. Use this for user-authorized access to Productboard API.

scopes.entities:read: Read access to entities (features, components, initiatives, etc.)
scopes.write:entities: Write access to create and modify entities

ResourceReferenceAssign.description:
Entity reference assignment using unique identifier for Entities.

## Behavior
- Simple ID-only assignment for referencing other entities
- Used for establishing relationships between entities (parent, child, link)
- UUID must match an existing entity in the workspace
- Commonly used in relationship creation and updates

ResourceReference.description:
# Description
A common response object of entity reference.

# Validation

# Filtering

Entity.description: The entity payload containing the resource data and its relationships.

EntityFieldValue.description:
A list of possible value types of PM entity fields.
This should cover all possible fields of all PM entity types.

EntityCreateOrUpdateFields.description:
A collection of fields that can be used to create or update a PM entity.
It is a subset of `EntityFields` that is used for creation or update operations.

## Dual Format Support
Most field types support two input formats:
- **Simple format**: Direct value (e.g., `"text"`, `123`, `true`)
- **Object format**: Value with metadata (e.g., `{"value": "text", "metadata": {...}}`)

## Metadata Support
Custom fields (identified by UUID) and specific field types (`domain`, `email`, `effort`) support 
optional metadata that allows tracking the external source of field values:
- `source.system`: Name of the external system that provided the value
- `source.recordId`: Identifier of the record in the external system
- `isViewableOnly`: Whether the value is read-only from external source

Metadata sent for other fields is ignored.

EntityCreateOrUpdateFieldValue.description: A list of create/update payloads for possible value types of a resource fields.

EntityPatch.description:
A list of patch operations to perform on a PM entity field.
Each operation can be one of `set`, `addItems`, `removeItems`, or `clear`.

EntityPatchOperation.description: This patch operation replaces the value of a field, or adds/removes values from it.

op.description:
The operation to perform on the field.
Can be one of `set`, `addItems`, `removeItems`.

path.description:
The path to the field to be modified.
It is usually a field id (eg. "tags", "owner", "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000").

EntityPatchOperationClear.description: This patch operation clears the value of a field.

op.description:
The operation to perform on the field.
Can be one of `clear`.

path.description:
The path to the field to be modified.
It is usually a field id (eg. "tags", "owner", "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000").

EntitySearch.description:
Request body for searching entities. Supports two formats:

**New format** (recommended): Uses a structured `filter` object with nested field groups.
**Legacy format** (deprecated): Uses flat properties directly under `data`.

Both formats are accepted. The legacy format will continue to work but is deprecated
in favour of the new structured format. Format is detected by the presence of the `filter` key.

EntitySearchFilter.description: Filter criteria for the entity search.

type.description: Filter by one or more entity types. If empty or not provided, searches across all entity types.

EntitySearchFieldFilters.description: Filters applied to entity field values.

customer.description: Filter by linked customer (not yet active).

EntitySearchMetadataFilter.description: Filters applied to entity metadata (not yet active).

DateRangeFilter.description: A date range filter with optional `from` and `to` bounds.

EntitySearchReturn.description: Controls which fields are included in the response.

fields.description:
Controls which fields are returned in the response to optimize bandwidth and tailor data to your needs.

**Options:**
- Not specified or `null`: Returns only fields with non-empty values
- `["all"]`: Returns all fields, including those with `null` values
- `["name", "status", ...]`: Returns only the specified fields

For detailed usage, examples, and field discovery, see [Response Field Control](https://developer.productboard.com/v2.0.0/reference/response-field-control).

A key-value definition fields that are part of the entity.
Key represents field id (eg. name, description, or UUID)

relationships.description:
A key-value definition of possible relationships to other entities.
Key represents the relationship type to other entities (child, parent, link)

filters.description:
Available filter parameters for this entity type.
Use these as query parameters on `GET /v2/entities` or as keys in `POST /v2/entities/search` request body.
Not all entity types support the same filters — this list is type-specific.

ConfigurationFilterParameter.description:
Describes a single filter parameter available for an entity type.
The `name` is the exact query parameter name to use when filtering entities.

name.description:
The query parameter name to use when filtering.
Use this exact name as a query parameter on `GET /v2/entities`
or as a key in the `POST /v2/entities/search` request body.

field.description: The field ID this filter applies to, when the filter is based on a field.

relationship.description: The relationship key this filter applies to, when the filter is based on a relationship.

requiredScopes.description:
OAuth2 scopes required to use this filter,
beyond the standard entity scopes. Omitted when no additional scopes are needed.

ConfigurationFilterSchema.description: Describes the expected value type and format for a filter parameter.

type.description: The value type expected for this filter.

format.description: Additional format hint for the value.

cardinality.description: Whether the entity can have single or multiple relationships of a given type

mandatory.description:
Specifies whether the entity must have such relationship to a target entity (e.g. it must have parent).
If a relationship is mandatory and can have multiple entity types as targets, then there must exist *at least
one* relationship to an entity that is of any of the specified types.

For example, a feature must have a parent that is either a component, or a product.

ConfigurationFieldLifecycle.description:
Defines the available operations for a field during different lifecycle stages.
Possible lifecycle stages are `create`, `update` and `patch`.
  - `create` defines what operations are allowed when creating an entity
  - `update` defines what operations are allowed when using a simple patch via `data.fields`
  - `patch` defines what operations are allowed when using a JSON Patch via `data.patch` array

ConfigurationFieldLifecycleOperation.description: Defines the specific operations allowed during a lifecycle stage.

set.description: Whether the field value can be set (assigned).

clear.description: Whether the field value can be cleared (set to null/empty).

addItems.description: Whether items can be added to the field (for list-type fields).

removeItems.description: Whether items can be removed from the field (for list-type fields).

ConfigurationFieldConstraints.description: Validation constraints and rules for the field.

maxSize.description: Maximum size in bytes for content fields.

maxItems.description: Maximum number of items for list-type fields.

allowEmpty.description: Whether empty string values are allowed.

uniqueItems.description: Whether items in list-type fields must be unique.

ConfigurationFieldConstraintsValues.description: Defines constraints on allowed values for the field.

ConfigurationFieldConstraintsQuery.description: Query-based value constraints referencing external endpoints.

list.description: URL endpoint that provides the list of allowed values.

ConfigurationFieldDefaultValue.description: Default value configuration for the field.

FieldValues.description:
Configuration for field values. Provides inline data for small sets
and/or a reference link for large/dynamic sets.

Interpretation:
- `data` only → Complete set inline
- `data` + `links.next` → Preview in data, full list at link
- `data` empty + `links.next` → Fully dynamic, fetch from link

data.description:
Inline value options. Can be empty.
For small sets: contains all values.
For large sets: contains preview or empty.

next.description:
URL to fetch values. Present only when more values exist
beyond what's in `data`. Supports pagination via `pageCursor`.

FieldValueItem.description: A single allowed value for a select-type or status field.

assignedEntityTypes.description: Entity types this value is assigned to. Only present for status field values.

examples.summary: Status field value with assigned entity types

ConfigurationField.description:
Field configuration with inline JSON schema and path for API operations.
This structure is used for all fields, including nested properties.

id.description:
The unique identifier of the field. For simple fields like "name", 
for nested fields like "timeframe.startDate", for UUID custom fields.

name.description: The display name of the field.

path.description: The path used for API operations. Uses "/fields/" prefix and nested paths for properties.

schema.description: Inline JSON Schema that defines this field's structure.

EntityRelationships.description:
A collection of relationships between this entity and other entities.
The list is paginated, to see the rest of the relationships follow the URL contained in the
`links.next` attribute.

EntityRelationship.description:
Represents a relationship between two entities.
The source entity is implicit from the request context (the entity being queried).

EntityRelationshipType.description:
The type of the relationship to be established.

## Relationships
- `parent`: Hierarchical relationship indicating the entity is a child of the target
- `child`: Hierarchical relationship indicating the entity contains the target
- `link`: Generic bidirectional relationship without semantic meaning
- `isBlockedBy`: Dependency relationship indicating the entity cannot proceed until the target is resolved
- `isBlocking`: Dependency relationship indicating the entity prevents progress on the target

EntityRelationshipCreate.description: Payload representing a new relationship towards target entity.

EntityType.description:
Supported Entity types:
  - product
  - component
  - feature
  - subfeature
  - initiative
  - objective
  - keyResult
  - release
  - releaseGroup
  - company
  - user

The exact types available may vary based on the configuration of the workspace.

FieldReference.description:
# Description
A common response object of field reference.

ApiSource.description:
Value sourced from an external API or integration.

## Behavior
- `system`: Optional display name for the external system (e.g., "Salesforce", "Jira")
- `recordId`: Optional combined ID in format `com.organization:recordId` (e.g., `com.salesforce:acc-12345`)
- `url`: Optional URL linking to the record in the external system

ValueMetadata.description:
Metadata associated with a field value, including its source and visibility settings.

## Behavior
- `source`: Origin of the value from external API
- `isViewableOnly`: If true, value cannot be edited through the API (read-only)

isViewableOnly.description: If true, this value is read-only and cannot be modified

EntityMetadata.description: Metadata associated with an entity, including its source.

UUIDFieldValue.description:
Universally unique identifier (UUID) for Entities.

## Behavior
- Validates for proper UUID structure and format
- Used for entity IDs and references to other entities
- Case-insensitive but typically lowercase

UUIDFieldValueWithMetadata.description: UUID value with optional metadata about its source and visibility.

TextFieldValue.description:
Short text content for Entities with a 255 character limit.

## Behavior
- Accepts any string content up to 255 characters
- Can be empty string (minLength: 0)
- Supports UTF-8 encoding for international characters
- Used for short descriptions, labels, identifiers, and status messages

TextFieldValueWithMetadata.description: Text value with optional metadata about its source and visibility.

NumberFieldValue.description:
Numeric values for quantitative data in Entities.

## Behavior
- Accepts both integers and floating-point numbers
- Supports positive, negative, and zero values
- No inherent minimum or maximum constraints (field-specific limits may apply)
- Used for effort estimation, scores, ratings, budgets, and metrics

NumberFieldValueWithMetadata.description:
Numeric value with optional metadata about its source and visibility.

## Usage
Number fields can be set using either:
- Simple format: `"effort": 5`
- Wrapped value format: `"effort": {"value": 5, "metadata": {...}}`

Use the object format when you need to track the source of the value or mark it as read-only.

NumberFieldValueMode.description:
Whether the number field value is calculated automatically or entered manually.
- `manual`: The field displays and uses the manually entered value
- `calculated`: The field displays and uses an automatically calculated value (e.g., from child entities)

NumberValueModeUpdate.description:
Update the calculation mode of a number field without changing its value.
- When switching to `calculated`, the system will compute the value automatically
- When switching to `manual`, the previously set manual value will be used

NumberFieldValueWithMode.description:
Numeric value with its calculation mode, returned when reading entities.
- `value`: The numeric value (can be null)
- `mode`: Indicates whether the value is `manual` (entered by user) or `calculated` (computed automatically)

## When This Is Returned
The API returns this format when the number field has mode information available.
For simple cases without mode tracking, the API returns a plain number (see `NumberFieldValue`).

RichTextFieldValue.description:
HTML-formatted text content for Entities.

## Behavior
- Accepts HTML-formatted strings up to 524KB (524,288 characters)
- Can be empty string (minLength: 0)
- Supports rich formatting including bold, italic, links, lists
  - Follow [Rich Text Formatting guidelines](https://developer.productboard.com/reference/rich-text-formatting)
- Used for detailed descriptions, specifications, documentation, and formatted comments

RichTextFieldValueWithMetadata.description: HTML-formatted text content with optional metadata about its source and visibility.

DateFieldValue.description:
Calendar date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) for Entities.

## Behavior
- Must follow ISO 8601 date format: YYYY-MM-DD
- Date-only format without time component
- Validates for proper date structure and valid calendar dates
- Used for due dates, milestones, release dates, and timeframe boundaries
- Timezone-independent (date only)

DateFieldValueWithMetadata.description: Calendar date with optional metadata about its source and visibility.

GranularityFieldValue.description:
Time granularity level for timeframe display and interpretation.

## Behavior
- Must be one of: `year`, `quarter`, `month`, `day`
- Affects how timeframe dates are displayed and interpreted in the UI
- Used in conjunction with TimeframeFieldValue to specify precision level
- Supports different planning scales from daily sprints to annual strategies
- Exact string matching for filtering

DateTimeFieldValue.description:
Date and time in ISO 8601 format for Entities.

## Behavior
- Must follow ISO 8601 datetime format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ
- Includes both date and time components with timezone information
- Validates for proper datetime structure and valid values
- Used for timestamps, creation dates, and precise time tracking

URLFieldValue.description:
Valid URL for Entities.

## Behavior
- Must be a valid URL following URI format standards
- Supports various protocols (http, https, ftp, etc.)
- Validates for proper URL structure and format
- Used for external links, documentation references, and resource URLs

URLFieldValueWithMetadata.description: URL value with optional metadata about its source and visibility.

NameFieldValue.description:
Entity names and titles for Entities with a 255 character limit.

## Behavior
- Accepts any string content up to 255 characters
- Can be empty string, though typically entity names are required
- Supports UTF-8 encoding for international characters
- Used for entity titles, display labels, and primary identifiers

StatusFieldValue.description:
Status information for Entities with ID and display name.

## Behavior
- Contains both unique `id` (UUID) and human-readable `name`
- Used for workflow states, progress indicators, and entity classifications
- Both fields are required for complete status representation
- Supports filtering by status ID or name
- Status values are workspace-specific and configurable

StatusFieldAssign.description:
Status assignment allowing identification by ID or name.

## Behavior
- Supports two identification methods: unique ID (UUID) or human-readable name
- Use ID for precision and consistency across API calls
- Use name for convenience when ID is unknown
- Names must match existing status values in workspace configuration

StatusFieldAssignById.description:
Status assignment using unique identifier.

## Behavior
- Identifies status by UUID for precision and consistency
- Preferred method for API integrations and automated workflows
- UUID must match an existing status in workspace configuration

StatusFieldAssignByName.description:
Status assignment using human-readable name.

## Behavior
- Identifies status by display name for convenience
- Useful when status ID is unknown or in manual workflows
- Name must exactly match an existing status in workspace configuration
- Case-sensitive string matching

MemberFieldValue.description:
Workspace member information.

## Behavior
- Contains both unique `id` (UUID) and `email` for member identification
- Used for ownership, assignment, and team member references
- Both fields are required for complete member representation
- Email serves as human-readable identifier

MemberFieldAssign.description:
Member assignment allowing identification by ID or email address.

## Behavior
- Supports two identification methods: unique ID (UUID) or email address
- Use ID for precision and consistency across API calls
- Use email for convenience when ID is unknown
- Email must match existing member in workspace

MemberAssignById.description:
Member assignment using unique identifier.

## Behavior
- Identifies member by UUID for precision and consistency
- Preferred method for API integrations and automated workflows
- UUID must match an existing member in workspace

MemberAssignByEmail.description:
Member assignment using email address.

## Behavior
- Identifies member by email address for convenience
- Useful when member ID is unknown or in manual workflows
- Email must match an existing member in workspace
- Must be valid email format

TeamsFieldValue.description:
Collection of teams associated with Entities.

## Behavior
- Array of TeamFieldValue objects, each containing team ID and name
- Can be empty array if no teams are assigned
- Used for multi-team assignments and collaborations
- Supports adding/removing individual teams via patch operations

TeamFieldValue.description:
Individual team information with ID and display name.

## Behavior
- Contains both unique `id` (UUID) and human-readable `name`
- Used within TeamsFieldValue arrays for team assignments
- Both fields are required for complete team representation
- Team values are workspace-specific and configurable
- Supports filtering by team ID or name

TeamFieldAssign.description:
Team assignment allowing identification by ID or name.

## Behavior
- Supports two identification methods: unique ID (UUID) or human-readable name
- Use ID for precision and consistency across API calls
- Use name for convenience when ID is unknown
- Names must match existing team values in workspace configuration

TeamAssignById.description:
Team assignment using unique identifier.

## Behavior
- Identifies team by UUID for precision and consistency
- Preferred method for API integrations and automated workflows
- UUID must match an existing team in workspace configuration

TeamAssignByName.description:
Team assignment using human-readable name.

## Behavior
- Identifies team by display name for convenience
- Useful when team ID is unknown or in manual workflows
- Name must exactly match an existing team in workspace configuration
- Case-sensitive string matching

TeamsFieldAssign.description:
Collection of team assignments allowing identification by ID or name.

## Behavior
- Array of team assignments, each identified by ID or name
- Can be empty array if no teams are assigned
- Each item can use either ID (for precision) or name (for convenience)
- All referenced teams must exist in workspace configuration
- Supports adding/removing individual teams via patch operations

TimeframeFieldValue.description:
Time period with start and end dates and granularity level for Entities.

This object contains:
- A `startDate` marking the beginning of the timeframe
- An `endDate` marking the conclusion of the timeframe
- A `granularity` indicating the time precision level (`year`, `quarter`, `month`, `day`)

## Behavior
- Both `startDate` and `endDate` use ISO 8601 date format (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `endDate` must be on or after `startDate`
- `granularity` affects how dates are displayed and interpreted in the UI
- All fields are optional, allowing partial timeframe specification
- Used for planning, scheduling, time-based filtering, and defining work periods for initiatives, features, and releases
- Supports different planning granularities from daily sprints to quarterly planning

## Examples

**Monthly planning timeframe:**
```json
{
  "startDate": "2024-01-01",
  "endDate": "2024-01-31",
  "granularity": "month"
}
```

**Quarterly initiative timeframe:**
```json
{
  "startDate": "2024-01-01",
  "endDate": "2024-03-31",
  "granularity": "quarter"
}
```

**Annual strategic timeframe:**
```json
{
  "startDate": "2024-01-01",
  "endDate": "2024-12-31",
  "granularity": "year"
}
```

**Sprint timeframe:**
```json
{
  "startDate": "2024-01-15",
  "endDate": "2024-01-29",
  "granularity": "day"
}
```

TimeframeFieldValueWithMetadata.description: Timeframe value with metadata about its source and visibility.

SingleSelectFieldValue.description:
Single selection option with ID, name, and optional color for Entities.

## Behavior
- Contains unique `id`, human-readable `name`, and optional `color` for UI display
- Represents a single selected value from a predefined list of options
- Values must match field configuration options
- Used for status fields, priority levels, categories, and other single-choice selections

SingleSelectFieldAssign.description:
Single select option assignment allowing identification by ID or name.

## Behavior
- Supports two identification methods: unique ID or human-readable name
- Use ID for precision and consistency across API calls
- Use name for convenience when ID is unknown
- Values must match existing options in field configuration

SingleSelectFieldAssignById.description:
Single select option assignment using unique identifier.

## Behavior
- Identifies select option by UUID for precision and consistency
- Preferred method for API integrations and automated workflows
- UUID must match an existing option in field configuration

SingleSelectFieldAssignByName.description:
Single select option assignment using human-readable name.

## Behavior
- Identifies select option by display name for convenience
- Useful when option ID is unknown or in manual workflows
- Name must exactly match an existing option in field configuration
- Case-sensitive string matching with length constraints

MultiSelectFieldValue.description:
Collection of multiple selection options for Entities.

## Behavior
- Array of SingleSelectFieldValue objects, each with ID, name, and optional color
- Can be empty array if no options are selected
- All values must match field configuration options
- Used for multi-category selections, tag-like fields, and multiple choice attributes
- Supports adding/removing individual options via patch operations

MultiSelectFieldAssign.description:
Collection of multi-select option assignments allowing identification by ID or name.

## Behavior
- Array of select option assignments, each identified by ID or name
- Can be empty array if no options are selected
- Each item can use either ID (for precision) or name (for convenience)
- All referenced options must exist in field configuration
- Supports adding/removing individual options via patch operations

BooleanFieldValue.description:
True/false flag for binary state tracking in Entities.

## Behavior
- Accepts only `true` or `false` boolean values
- Used for binary states like archived status, feature toggles, completion flags
- Displayed as checkboxes or toggle switches in UI

BooleanFieldValueWithMetadata.description: Boolean value with optional metadata about its source and visibility.

HealthFieldValue.description:
Health status of a Entities with tracking metadata and history.

This object contains:
- A unique `id` for the health record
- The current `status` (`notSet`, `onTrack`, `atRisk`, `offTrack`)
- The calculation `mode` (`manual` or `calculated`)
- Timestamp and user tracking information
- Optional comment providing context
- Previous status for change tracking

## Behavior
- The `status` field indicates current health state
- The `mode` field determines how health is set:
  - `manual`: Status explicitly set by users with optional comments
  - `calculated`: Status automatically derived from system logic or child entities
- `lastUpdatedAt` is automatically maintained by the system
- `previousStatus` tracks the last health state for change detection
- `createdBy` identifies who set the health status (for manual mode)
- Read-only field populated by the system after health updates
- Used to monitor work item health across the product hierarchy and track status changes over time

## Examples

**Manual health with comment:**
```json
{
  "id": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000",
  "status": "atRisk",
  "mode": "manual",
  "lastUpdatedAt": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "comment": "Resource constraints affecting timeline",
  "createdBy": {
    "email": "manager@company.com"
  },
  "previousStatus": "onTrack"
}
```

**Calculated health:**
```json
{
  "id": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174001",
  "status": "onTrack",
  "mode": "calculated",
  "lastUpdatedAt": "2024-01-15T15:45:00Z",
  "previousStatus": "notSet"
}
```

lastUpdatedAt.description: The date and time when the health status was last updated.

comment.description: An optional comment providing additional context about the health status.

HealthUpdateFieldValue.description:
Represents the health status of a Entities, either manually set or automatically calculated.

This object contains:
- The mode of health tracking (`manual` or `calculated`)
- The health `status` (required only when mode is `manual`)
- An optional `comment` to provide context
- Metadata on who created the update (`createdBy`)

### Behavior
- If `mode` is `"manual"`, the `status` field is **required**, and you may include an optional `comment`.
- If `mode` is `"calculated"`, **do not provide** the `status` or `comment` fields – they will be ignored or cause validation errors.

### Use cases
- Set `manual` mode to explicitly assign a health status with reasoning.
- Use `calculated` mode when health is derived from internal logic or automation within Productboard.

### Examples

**Manual mode:**
```json
{
  "mode": "manual",
  "status": "atRisk",
  "comment": "Project timeline at risk due to resource constraints"
}
```

**Calculated mode:**
```json
{
  "mode": "calculated"
}
```

comment.description: An optional comment providing additional context about the health status.

value.comment: Project timeline at risk due to resource constraints

HealthUpdateFieldValueWithMetadata.description: Health update value with metadata about its source and visibility.

HealthStatusEnum.description: The health status of the work item.

HealthModeEnum.description: The mode of health status calculation.

ProgressFieldValue.description:
Measurable progress tracking for key results with start, target, and current values.

This object contains:
- A `startValue` representing the baseline measurement
- A `targetValue` representing the goal to achieve
- A `currentValue` representing the present measurement

## Behavior
- All values are floating-point numbers allowing decimal precision
- Values can represent various metrics (percentages, counts, amounts, etc.)
- The `currentValue` is updated to track progress toward the `targetValue`
- Progress calculation: `(currentValue - startValue) / (targetValue - startValue) * 100`
- Supports both positive and negative progress directions
- Currently supported only for key results
- Used to track quantifiable metrics like revenue, users, and performance indicators

## Examples

**Percentage-based progress:**
```json
{
  "startValue": 0.0,
  "targetValue": 100.0,
  "currentValue": 75.5
}
```

**Revenue tracking:**
```json
{
  "startValue": 50000.0,
  "targetValue": 100000.0,
  "currentValue": 87500.0
}
```

**User count growth:**
```json
{
  "startValue": 1000.0,
  "targetValue": 5000.0,
  "currentValue": 3250.0
}
```

startValue.description: The starting baseline value for progress measurement.

targetValue.description: The target goal value to achieve.

currentValue.description: The current progress value being tracked.

ProgressFieldValueWithMetadata.description: Progress value with metadata about its source and visibility.

startValue.description: The starting baseline value for progress measurement.

targetValue.description: The target goal value to achieve.

currentValue.description: The current progress value being tracked.

WorkProgressFieldValue.description:
Work progress percentage for Entities, tracking completion status from 0-100%.

This object contains:
- A numeric `value` representing completion percentage (0-100)
- A `mode` indicating how the progress is calculated (`manual`, `statusBased`, or `calculated`)

## Behavior
- The `value` field must be an integer between 0 and 100 (inclusive)
- The `mode` field determines how progress is tracked and updated:
  - `manual`: Progress is set directly by users
  - `statusBased`: Progress is automatically calculated based on entity status
  - `calculated`: Progress is derived from child entity relationships (parent/link)
- Different entity types support different modes:
  - **subfeature**: `manual`, `statusBased`
  - **feature**: `manual`, `statusBased`, `calculated`
  - **initiative**: `manual`, `calculated`
  - **objective**: `manual`, `calculated`
- When mode is `calculated`, the system automatically updates the value based on relationships
- Used to track completion percentage across the product hierarchy and monitor progress aggregation

## Examples

**Manual progress:**
```json
{
  "value": 75,
  "mode": "manual"
}
```

**Status-based progress:**
```json
{
  "value": 50,
  "mode": "statusBased"
}
```

**Calculated progress from relationships:**
```json
{
  "value": 85,
  "mode": "calculated"
}
```

value.description: The work progress percentage value between 0 and 100 (inclusive).

WorkProgressFieldValueWithMetadata.description: Work progress value with metadata about its source and visibility.

value.description: The work progress percentage value between 0 and 100 (inclusive).

WorkProgressModeEnum.description: The mode of work progress calculation.

ErrorSource.description: An object containing references to the source of the error. It helps with location of the problematic field or parameter.

pointer.description: JSON Pointer [RFC6901] to the associated entity in the request document.

parameter.description:
Query parameter causing the error.
Used when the error is related to a query parameter rather than request body.

Error.description: Individual error object containing the details and context.

code.description:
A unique, machine-readable and stable code that identifies this error.
Must be consistent across all services and provide clear categorization.
Format: `category.subcategory.specificError`

title.description:
A short, human-readable summary of the problem that doesn't change
from occurrence to occurrence of the problem. Should provide immediate
understanding of the error type.

detail.description:
A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.
May include suggestions for resolution but should not expose sensitive information.

id.description:
A unique identifier for this specific occurrence of the problem.
It carries unique request/response ID which allows client
and server to reference the exact instance of the error
in logs or support communication.

errors.description: Array of error objects, multiple errors can be returned.

Type.name: type
Type.description:
The type of the entity. Currently supported PM entity types:
  - product
  - component
  - feature
  - subfeature
  - initiative
  - objective
  - keyResult
  - release
  - releaseGroup
  - user
  - company

The exact types available may vary based on the configuration of the workspace.

Fields.name: fields[]
Fields.description:
Controls which fields are returned in the response to optimize bandwidth and tailor data to your needs.

**Options:**
- Not specified: Returns only fields with non-empty values (default)
- `fields[]=all`: Returns all fields, including those with `null` values
- `fields[]=name&fields[]=status`: Returns only the specified fields

For detailed usage, examples, and field discovery, see [Response Field Control](https://developer.productboard.com/v2.0.0/reference/response-field-control).
